title: acalypha eastmostpointensis
intro: Acalypha eastmostpointensis, also known as Eastmost Point Copperleaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a shrub-like plant with copper-colored leaves and is native to tropical regions. Acalypha eastmostpointensis has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant's leaves can be made into a tea or infused in oil for use in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments.
